#  Export RFIs to CSV or PDF 
##  Background 
The RFIs tool lets you export the records in your project's RFIs tool to two (2) file formats: CSV or PDF. You also have the choice between exporting the entire RFI list for a project or exporting the information in a single RFI.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * **Additional Information:**
    * The exported data reflects any active search results, filters, and sorted columns. See [Search for and Filter RFIs](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/search-for-and-filter-rfis).
    * If you want to download all of the RFIs for a project, users with the appropriate permissions can extract the RFI information using the Procore Extracts application.


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **RFIs** tool.
  2. Click **Export**.
  3. Choose one of the following options:
     * **PDF - All Responses**
     * **PDF - Official Only**
     * **PDF**
     * **CSV**  



## See Also
  * [RFIs - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/permissions)
  * [Export an RFI](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/rfi-project/tutorials/export-an-rfi)
  * [Which tools can I extract project data from using the Procore Extracts application?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-which-tools-can-i-extract-project-data-from-using-the-procore-extracts-application)
title: Export RFIs to CSV or PDF
description: How to export the records in a project's RFIs log to a Comma Separated Values (CSV) or Portable Document Format (PDF) file.
